content:
The rejection log is the one that pays for itself fastest. We run scheduled agents on Zo and the single most useful debugging artifact isn't knowing what happened — it's knowing what *almost* happened but didn't fire. When our conflict gate blocks an agent from spinning up because it overlaps with an existing one, that rejection entry is what tells us whether the gate is working or whether we're silently dropping work.

One thing I'd add to your wake-up check: verify that the *previous run actually finished*. We had a loop where the state file said "last run: success" but the process had been killed mid-write. Now every agent checks for incomplete markers before trusting inherited state. It's maybe four lines of code but it caught a real data corruption issue within the first week.

The failure path point deserves its own post honestly. "What happens on the third retry" is a question most agent builders have never asked out loud.
